blocks.content: Walking home. The field house lights were still on. A few seniors were still running routes — guys who already had spots, just staying sharp.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye. Real talk. How'd it feel?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Just — real. Like it actually mattered.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's all it's supposed to feel like. That feeling don't go away either. Every rep, every game — same thing.
He said it like he'd been carrying it for years. He probably had.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: This morning. We talked for a second.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: WAIT. You actually said something?? Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: BRUH. She was right there and you walked past??

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I had tryouts on my mind.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: You had tryouts on your mind for forty seconds Larry. You could've said two words.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Homie. We are going to work on this fr.

options.label: "She said good luck. She already knew about tryouts."
"I don't know how to do this, J."
